title: published ports not available
body:
### Issue Description

When i run container in podman and publish ports this ports not available on other machine. I can reach this ports on machine where podman is running. I run python3 http.server and reach this port, when container is down, so looks like it is not firewall issue.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman run -dt -p 8080:80 nginx


### Describe the results you received

When curl http://127.0.0.1:8080 from podman machine all works fine. When curl from another machine i can see tcp SYN but no SYN, ACK in reverse. 

### Describe the results you expected

I expect see nginx page in another machine

comments.body: Podman does not poke holes in firewalls.  If  you want ports available to remote machines, you have to adjust the firewall rules.
i open this port in ufw and can start python script to listen on this port

> Podman does not poke holes in firewalls. If you want ports available to remote machines, you have to adjust the firewall rules.

looks like it needs some special rules for cni firewall
i add rules in CNI-ADMIN and things start working, but is it should work like this?
Like i should open port and in addition add rules to specific chain?
If you want a process on a separate machine to reach a port on your host machine, then the port needs to be opened. Not really sure what your question means.
